# Cloud Waste Scanner Community v2.9.19

v2.9.19 completes the first source-visible Kubernetes/container scanning lane for Community users who need local-first infrastructure waste evidence without sending cluster data to a hosted service.

## What changed

- Added an explicit Kubernetes Local Cluster target in the scan wizard.
- Added kubeconfig path and optional context inputs for local kubectl scans.
- Expanded Kubernetes scans beyond pods, nodes, and PersistentVolumes to include workloads and LoadBalancer services.
- Added workload replica drift findings for deployments, statefulsets, and daemonsets with reserved CPU/memory signals.
- Added LoadBalancer service review findings for stale or incomplete service shapes.
- Added namespace and owner/team hints to Kubernetes workload and service evidence when labels are available.
- Expanded kubeconfig path handling so `~/.kube/config` works as the default UI value.

## Validation

- `npm run build`
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo test -p cloud-waste-scanner-core`
- `cargo test -p cloud-waste-scanner-gui --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`
- `cargo test --workspace`

## Compatibility

- App metadata version: `2.9.19`
- Kubernetes scanning uses local `kubectl` with read-only kubeconfig permissions.
- Direct kube-apiserver SDK mode and kind/minikube fixture tests remain future enhancements.
